title: Anatomy of an Ethereum Block
description: A visual breakdown of the core components that make up a block in the Ethereum blockchain, showing their hierarchical structure and relationships.
elements: Block Header (Parent Hash, State Root, etc.)
Consensus Layer Data (e.g., attestations for Proof-of-Stake)
relationships: The Block Header contains a cryptographic hash of the Transaction List
The State Root in the header is a hash of the global state, which is altered by executing transactions and smart contracts
Uncle Blocks are referenced by the main chain block to improve security and reduce centralization
Consensus data validates the block's agreement with the network rules
